chapter_context.summary: Pronounces severe judgment on the leadership of Jerusalem and Judah, detailing the societal collapse and the stripping away of the pride and luxurious ornaments of the daughters of Zion.

outline.text: Society Collapsing: The Removal of Rulers, Leaders, and Order
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Society Collapsing: The Removal of Rulers, Leaders, and Order (3:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Denouncing Unjust Rulers and the Oppression of the Poor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Denouncing Unjust Rulers and the Oppression of the Poor (3:8-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Judgment on the Luxurious Pride of the Daughters of Zion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judgment on the Luxurious Pride of the Daughters of Zion (3:16-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 3, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
